topics: Classification and Efficiency of Surface Irrigation Methods: Border Irrigation - Design Parameters, Evaluation, and Ideal Wetting Pattern; Furrow Irrigation - Design Parameters, Types of Furrows, Evaluation, and Ideal Wetting Pattern; Basin Irrigation - Types of Basins, Ideal Wetting Pattern, Shapes, and Size

Crop Water Requirements: Infiltration and Movement of Water in Soil; Soil-Water-Plant Relationship; Water Requirement of Crops; Evapotranspiration (ET) and Consumptive Use; Effective Rainfall; Irrigation Requirement; Soil Water Balance; Yield Response to Water; Production Functions

Irrigation Water Distribution: Canal Network and Canal Regulation; Methods of Distribution - Supply Based and Demand Based; Delivery of Water to Farms; Measurement of Water; Scheduling of Irrigation - Criteria and Constraints; Frequency and Interval of Irrigation
Systems Classification: Rehabilitation and Modernization, Performance Indicators, Improving System Performance, and Constraints
Land Drainage Systems: Necessity, Types, Surface and Subsurface Drainage, and Design Considerations

Soil Water Zone: Description and Flow Through Soil Water Zone; Physical Properties of Soil - Hydraulic Conductivity, Saturated Thickness, Drainable Pore Space, Storativity, Hydraulic Resistance, and Leakage Factor
Ground Water Data: Concepts of Ground Water Hydrograph, Ground Water Maps, Isobath Map, and Water Table Fluctuation Maps
Drainage Studies: Continuity Equation, Laplace Equation, and Relaxation Method of Solution
Typical Boundary Conditions: Impervious Layer, Plane of Symmetry, Free Water Surface, Water at Rest or Slowly Moving Water, and Seepage Surface
Dupit-Forchheimer Theory: Steady Flow Above an Impervious Horizontal Boundary - Dupit's Equation and Water Table Subject to Recharge
Flow into Open Drains: Steady State Equations - Hooghoudt Equation, Principles, Applications for Design, and Use of Nomographs for Homogeneous and Layered Soils; Earnst Equation
Concept of Horizontal, Vertical, and Radial Flow and Application to Layered Soils

Unsteady State Drainage Equations: Glover-Dum Equation - Application; Concept of Kraijenhoff Vande Leur Mass Land Equation - Application and Analysis for Constant Recharge and Intermittent Recharge Cases
Layout of Open Drainage Systems: Types - Field Drains, Design Considerations of Ditch Drains, Mole Drains - Design Considerations and Suitability
Sub-surface Drainage Systems: Pipe Drainage Systems Design for Uniform and Non-uniform Flow Conditions, Transport, and Dewatering Situations

Patterns of Drainage System and Drainage Criteria Formulation: Off Season Drainage, Crop Season Drainage, and Salt Drainage; Use of Steady State and Unsteady State Approaches in Formulation; Criteria for Irrigated Area and Incorporation of Intentional and Unavoidable Losses

Salinity and Drainage: Cause of Salinity, Salt Balance Equation, Leaching Efficiency, Salt Equilibrium Equation, and Leaching Requirement; Salt Storage Equation; Expressing Equations in Electrical Conductivity Terms; Design of a Drainage System for an Irrigated Area Based on Crop Water Requirement and Leaching Requirement; Dynamic Equilibrium Concept
Gravity Outlet Structures: Types and Location
